expectations.evidence: Issue 1 titled 'AI Purple Gradient — Instant Commoditization' explicitly identifies '#8B5CF6-to-#EC4899 is the default gradient of AI landing pages' as High severity, referencing the Universal Anti-Patterns rule

expectations.text: Flags Inter at 400 weight on muted palette as generic/timid
expectations.evidence: Issue 3 titled 'Inter 400 on Muted Gray — Legibility Failure' flags it as High severity, noting 'Inter at weight 400 is already a light-reading font. Pair it with muted gray text on a purple-to-pink gradient background, and you have a contrast problem'

expectations.text: Flags centered text spanning full 1440px as too wide for center alignment
expectations.evidence: Issue 4 titled 'Full-Width Text at 1440px — Unreadable Lines' states 'At 1440px container width with 18px text, you are looking at 160+ characters per line' and recommends 'Constrain the text block to max-width 680px'

expectations.text: Flags stock photo of people in office
expectations.evidence: Issue 5 titled 'Stock Office Photo — Generic and Trust-Eroding' states 'A stock photo of people in an office communicates nothing about the product. It is the visual equivalent of lorem ipsum.'

expectations.text: Flags 12px body text as below 14px minimum
expectations.evidence: Issue 2 identifies the broken type scale with '12px body text fails accessibility standards' and states 'Body text minimum 16px (Inter 400). For a hero section specifically, 18px is the floor'

expectations.text: Suggests specific alternative choices (different gradient, font, alignment)
expectations.evidence: Report provides specific alternatives: for gradient 'Pick one brand color. Build a tonal palette'; for typography 'Inter 600 at 48px heading, Inter 400 at 18px body'; for layout 'max-width 680px centered'; for image 'Product screenshot or UI mockup'
